_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 8-15 Interfacility Patient Transfers (Revised 3/3/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan System Protocol Section 8-15: Interfacility Patient Transfers — MDHHS Approved 3/3/2025
purpose: Govern the requirements for interfacility patient transfers including staffing, scope of practice, and special patient populations. The staffing and licensure level of the transporting EMS unit must be appropriate for the patient's condition and the interventions required during transport. MCAs must define which transfer types are permitted and required staffing levels.

sections.title: General Interfacility Transfer Requirements
points: The transporting EMS unit must have the scope of practice and equipment appropriate for the patient's condition during transport
Sending facility must provide a complete verbal and written report to the transporting EMS crew before departure
The transporting crew must review the sending facility's orders and assess the patient's condition before accepting the transfer
If the patient's condition exceeds the transporting crew's scope of practice, refuse the transfer and request an appropriately staffed unit or hospital personnel accompaniment
EMS may decline a transfer that is clinically unsafe — contact Medical Control and notify MCA if needed

sections.title: Staffing Requirements by Patient Condition
points: BLS TRANSPORT: patient is hemodynamically stable; no ALS interventions in place or anticipated; no advanced airway; IV saline lock only (no medications running)
ALS TRANSPORT: IV medications running; advanced airway in place; hemodynamically unstable or at risk; ALS monitoring required (cardiac monitor, capnography)
PEEP > 5 cmH2O on mechanical ventilation: requires MICU, air medical, or hospital staff accompaniment per Section 7-27
HFNO (High-Flow Nasal Oxygen): requires Paramedic with MCA authorization per Section 7-26
Enhanced Paramedic (EP) or Critical Care Paramedic (CCP): refer to Section 8-15(S) for additional scope authorizations

sections.title: Medications and Drips During Transfer
points: EMS providers may continue facility-initiated IV medication infusions within their scope of practice during interfacility transfer — providers are NOT starting new medications, they are continuing facility-ordered therapy
Paramedics may continue: standard cardiac medications (antiarrhythmics, vasopressors at maintenance rates), heparin, insulin per sending facility orders
BLS/Specialist providers may continue: normal saline, LR, or D5W infusions at ordered rates; may NOT continue vasoactive or ALS medication infusions
If an IV medication problem occurs en route: follow the appropriate protocol for the patient's condition; contact Medical Control
Blood products: require Enhanced Paramedic or Critical Care Paramedic with MCA authorization per Section 8-15(S)

sections.title: HFNO — Interfacility Authorization
points: MCA OPTIONAL: MCA may authorize Paramedics with appropriate training to continue HFNO during interfacility transport
Requires MCA selection in BOTH this protocol AND Section 7-26 (HFNO Procedure Protocol)
Refer to Section 7-26 for HFNO administration requirements

sections.title: Documentation
points: Document sending facility name and address, receiving facility name and address, and reason for transfer
Document patient condition at time of accepting transfer and at time of delivery to receiving facility
Document all medications running during transfer and any changes in patient condition en route
Copy of sending facility's transfer orders should accompany patient

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must also select HFNO authorization in Section 7-26 (HFNO Procedure Protocol) in addition to this selection
options: Authorized — Paramedic with MCA-approved HFNO training

mca_selections_required.description: MCA may define which vasoactive medications Paramedics may continue during interfacility transfer
options: Paramedic may continue MCA-defined vasoactive medications per sending facility orders
Paramedic may continue all standard vasoactive medications per sending facility orders
Contact Medical Control for vasoactive medications during transfer

deterioration_flags: Patient condition exceeds transporting crew scope of practice — decline transfer; request appropriately staffed unit or hospital accompaniment; contact Medical Control
Patient deteriorates during interfacility transport — contact Medical Control; manage per appropriate protocol; consider diverting to closest appropriate facility
scope_notes: Transporting unit scope must match patient condition and interventions required
BLS: stable, no ALS interventions, saline lock only
ALS: IV medications, advanced airway, hemodynamic instability
HFNO: Paramedic + MCA authorization (both Section 8-15 and 7-26)
Blood products: EP/CCP with MCA authorization only
EMS may decline unsafe transfers — contact Medical Control
